target.title: Telegram markdown rendering: '$' followed by digit/uppercase triggers variable interpolation, inline '**bold**' causes font inconsistency, line-start '#' triggers H1 heading
target.body:
## Summary

When sending messages via the `message` tool to Telegram, hand-written markdown text containing certain patterns triggers unintended Telegram MarkdownV2 rendering, causing **font size inconsistencies** within a single message:

1. **`$` followed by a digit or uppercase letter** (e.g. `$100`, `$go`) — when not wrapped in backticks, Telegram MarkdownV2 may interpret it as a variable or italic token, changing the font of the entire surrounding line.
2. **Inline `**bold**` mixed with regular text in the same line** — produces a font size mismatch within the same line (large bold + small regular).
3. **`#` followed by a digit at line start** (e.g. `#12345`) — Telegram MarkdownV2 parses it as an H1 heading (huge font), making that single line visually out of place.

## Reproduction

Send a `message` tool call to a Telegram channel with textMode `markdown` (default) containing:

```
- Stocks position: Entry $xxx.xx / TP $xxx.xxx / SL $xxx.xx
- A: bold warning **entered**, B: still watching
#12345 user feedback: rendering issue
```

Observed on Telegram (Android + Desktop):
- Line 1: `$731.38` triggers italic / variable interpolation — whole line is italic, while surrounding lines are regular.
- Line 2: `**entered**` is bold (large), the rest of the line is regular (small) — visible font jump mid-line.
- Line 3: `#12345` is rendered as a huge H1 heading, while the other lines are normal text.

## Root Cause

Telegram MarkdownV2 special characters (per Telegram Bot API): `_*[]()~\`>#+-=|{}.!`

- `$` is **NOT** in the MarkdownV2 escape list — so `\$` is not a valid escape. `$` followed by digit/uppercase still triggers variable interpolation in the parser.
- The escape patterns that work for Python f-strings (e.g. `\$`, `\#`) do **not** help here — those are Python-level escapes, not Telegram MarkdownV2 escapes.
- `**bold**` rendering depends on `parse_mode`: under `markdownv2` it is not recognized (raw `**` may show or be dropped); under legacy `markdown` it works but mixing bold with non-bold within one line is visually jarring.
- `#` at line start, followed by a space or digit, is parsed as an H1 heading by MarkdownV2 (and partially by legacy `markdown`).

## Current Workaround

The user is expected to self-lint message text before sending. Known rules that avoid the issue:
1. Wrap `$` followed by digit/uppercase in backticks (`` `$100` ``, `` `$go` ``), or use plain text instead of the `$` symbol.
2. Avoid inline `**bold**` mixed with regular text — use emoji (`⚠️`, `✅`, `🟡`) or place bold on its own line / heading.
3. Never start a line with `#` followed by a digit — describe the reference in words instead of as a Markdown heading.

This is fragile: every agent prompt and every developer writing hand-written `message` text must remember these rules. There is no tool-side guard.

## Suggested Improvements

Add a pre-send check (lint) in the `message` tool that warns the caller when:
- `$` followed by digit/uppercase is found outside backticks.
- `**bold**` appears mid-line (not as a complete line).
- Line starts with `#` followed by space/digit (potential heading).

Possible implementations:
- A new `--lint-telegram` flag on `message` that runs the checks and refuses to send (or returns a warning payload) if risky patterns are found.
- A `--auto-escape-telegram` flag that automatically wraps risky patterns in backticks before sending.
- Documentation in the `message` tool help / docs describing the rendering rules.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: latest
- Channel: `telegram`
- `textMode`: `markdown` (default)
- Affected call site: hand-written agent replies via the `message` tool

## Related Issues

- #94117 — Telegram rich final reply URL handling
- #91383 — Telegram normal reply can start mid-URL / lose opening text
- #55512 — Markdown table columns misaligned with CJK
target.author: Hansen1018
